message:
feat(legacy): add preview ABI entrypoints for malloc, free, strlen, strcmp, memset

Extends the legacy glibc_rust preview crate with additional TSM-mediated
entrypoints that exercise the original membrane registry (separate from
the new glibc-rs-membrane pipeline, kept for migration compatibility):

- glibc_rust_malloc_preview(size): allocates via system allocator,
  registers with membrane registry, returns null on zero-size or
  layout error. Layout uses 16-byte alignment.

- glibc_rust_free_preview(ptr, size_hint): classifies pointer via
  membrane registry, handles Known/Foreign/Freed states with
  appropriate membrane state transitions and metric recording.

- glibc_rust_strlen_preview(s): scans for null terminator with
  configurable max scan length (1MB default), records validation
  metrics. Returns 0 for null input.

- glibc_rust_strcmp_preview(s1, s2): byte-by-byte comparison with
  null-terminator awareness, returns negative/zero/positive per C
  strcmp contract. Handles null inputs gracefully (null < non-null).

- glibc_rust_memset_preview(dest, c, n): fills n bytes with value c,
  validates destination pointer via membrane classify_pointer(),
  records metrics. Returns dest pointer per C memset contract.

All entrypoints are #[unsafe(no_mangle)] extern "C" with the
glibc_rust_*_preview naming convention to avoid symbol conflicts with
the production glibc-rs-abi exports. Added clippy::missing_safety_doc
allow at crate level (membrane validates at runtime).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
